audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in params.c are safe for known callers: function parameters (info, name, value) are assert-guarded and all callers provide non-NULL values (config.c:283 checks argc>=3; map.c:454 validates map->lm_name non-NULL at parse time per lines 304-307; val is a local struct asserted non-NULL at map.c:373). The tree invariant ensures var->lv_value.bv_val is non-NULL for all successfully inserted params; an OOM-induced NULL bv_val in rewrite_param_set (line 49 strdup failure) would propagate to rewrite_param_get but requires memory exhaustion, not direct attacker input.
